title: JavaScript Loop Control Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of for, while, and do-while loops in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about a for loop?
options: It always runs exactly once.
It's typically used when the number of iterations is known before entering the loop.
It cannot be exited prematurely.
It's primarily used for objects only.

questions.question: What is the key difference between while and do-while loops?
options: They have no difference.
A do-while loop's code block always runs at least once before checking the condition.
A while loop's code block always runs at least once.
A do-while loop is only used for numerical conditions.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'break' statement within a loop?
options: To skip the current iteration and move to the next one.
To restart the current iteration.
To terminate the entire loop prematurely.
To pause the script for 1 second.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct syntax for a for...of loop over an array named 'items'?
options: for (item items) {}
for (let item in items) {}
for (item of items) {}
for (let item of items) {}

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'continue' statement in a loop?
options: To exit the entire script.
To skip the rest of the current iteration and proceed to the next iteration of the loop.
To restart the script.
To wait for a network request to complete.
